description: Chairs, stools, benches, and sitting cushions all fall under 'meipa', an object or prepared place made for sitting. No one furniture form is the default. 'nuwera' (bed) supports lying down for extended rest.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm' and open into mid-front 'e'. Keep a clear hiatus into stressed high-front 'i', then close both lips for unvoiced 'p' and release into open 'a'.
sound_symbolism: The opening 'mei' recalls 'meilo' (sit), giving the object and the posture an audible Phi kinship. The final stop makes the short word settle firmly.
usage_notes: Back, height, material, and mobility can distinguish a particular seat. A seat can be reserved or exalted, but the noun itself is only somewhere to sit.

semantic_domains.activity: a place prepared for the bodily posture of sitting
semantic_domains.physical: an object or surface used as a seat
